articles.title: Walmart-backed Flipkart expands quick-commerce push as Amazon ramps up in India
articles.summary: Walmart-backed Flipkart has crossed 1,000 micro-fulfillment centers as Amazon accelerates its own quick-commerce push in India.

articles.title: India’s MoEngage bets that the future of marketing is millions of AI agents
articles.summary: The all-cash deal gives MoEngage access to technology that assigns AI agents to individual customers.

articles.title: Superhuman acquires AI detection startup GPTZero
articles.summary: Superhuman, which also has an AI detection tool as part of Grammarly, has snapped up GPTZero.

articles.title: After betting the firm on Anthropic, Menlo Ventures raises victorious $3B fund
articles.summary: Menlo has created a solid rep for itself as an AI investor, all based on one gutsy $750 million move in 2024.

articles.title: Klue says hackers stole credential from 2022 that led to customer data breaches
articles.summary: It's unclear why Klue had not revoked the credential after the limited pilot, which hackers then used to breach a system holding keys for accessing customers' data.

articles.title: Mark Zuckerberg wants Meta to launch its own prediction market
articles.summary: The app would be independent of Meta's other social media offerings, although sources told the NYT that those social sites could direct users to engagement with the app.

articles.title: Anthropic’s Claude Tag is learning your company, one Slack message at a time
articles.summary: Anthropic’s new Claude Tag brings an always-on AI teammate to Slack. But beyond productivity, the feature is a strategic play to capture organizational context, institutional knowledge, and enterprise workflows.

articles.title: Password manager maker LastPass says hackers stole customer support case data during Klue breach
articles.summary: This is the second data breach to affect LastPass customers in recent years, after one of the password manager's tech partners was recently breached.

articles.title: Anthropic Backer Menlo Ventures Raises $3B In New Funds To Back AI Startups Across Stages
articles.summary: Venture firm Menlo Ventures has raised $3 billion across two new funds, the largest capital raise in its 50-year history, to invest in AI startups from seed through growth stage. The firm says the new capital will target companies in sectors from enterprise tools to healthcare.

articles.title: As 93% of Americans lean on coupons to get by, Bed Bath & Beyond is splashing out $100K on a home renovation for their thriftiest shopper
articles.summary: Bed Bath & Beyond’s “Legendary Coupon Hunt” is underway, and the thriftiest shopper will get a $100,000 home makeover if they bring in the oldest coupon to date.

articles.title: MSCI delays Indonesia’s market status review until November
articles.summary: The move is likely to deepen investor unease that’s built over months after MSCI in January flagged a potential downgrade to frontier status.

articles.title: Quantum computing stocks surge after Trump signed executive orders backing the sector
articles.summary: While the tech-heavy Nasdaq fell more than 2%, some quantum computing stocks surged by double digits.

articles.title: Anthropic engineering head says Claude Code made employees’ work a ‘lonely experience’—and it could hint at Big Tech’s bigger morale problem
articles.summary: Tech workers are some of AI’s biggest users and supporters, but the people using the tech the most may also be experiencing the most anxiety about it.

articles.title: The climate policy triangle: why leaders can no longer choose between growth, security and sustainability
articles.summary: The old trade-off framing is obsolete. The next decade's defining challenge is making growth, strategic autonomy, and sustainability reinforce each other.

articles.title: Amazon Prime Day isn’t a midsummer shopping event anymore. Here’s what changed in 2026
articles.summary: Amazon pulled Prime Day forward to June 23 this year, racing to reach shoppers' wallets before summer travel, the World Cup, and rival retailers' competing sales.

articles.title: The hidden cost of your AI rollout: burning out the high performers running it
articles.summary: Companies are leaning on their best people to lead the AI charge. A Wellhub executive explains why that strategy could backfire—and what HR is doing about it.

articles.title: The U.S. cut cancer deaths by 34% since 1991—but not in 458 rural counties
articles.summary: The richest counties improved at 7x the rate of the poorest. Cancer mortality is actually up in the countryside.

articles.title: 仙工智能的过山车：暗盘破发7%、首日冲高38%
articles.summary:
本文约1100字，建议阅读3分钟

作者 | 彭孝秋

6月24日，仙工智能（06106.HK）正式在港交所敲钟上市，开盘平开，报101.6港元，随后一路拉升，最高冲到140.50（+38.3%）。之后回落，截至发稿报115.70港元，仍涨13.88%。振幅38.29%，成交5.33亿，市值127.8亿港元。

articles.title: AI × OPC的新可能：一个人，就是一支队伍｜2026WAVES
articles.summary:
2026年6月16日，36氪2026 WAVES 新浪潮大会在广州番禺良仓新造创意园开幕。以下这场圆桌论坛是大会首日下午的收官议题，探讨 AI 时代 OPC（One Person Company）的真实生态。

八九十年代，一辆三轮车、一个摊位，就能撑起一门生意。今天的 OPC 看起来形似，但底层逻辑已经彻底不同。

过去个体户的杠杆是体力、经验和地理位置，本质上是基于本地的 LBS 服务，脱不开那个具体的摊位。今天 OPC 的杠杆是代码和媒体——边际成本几乎为零的杠杆，加上 AI 带来的生产力平权，让一个人第一次拥有了接近公司的交付能力。

<

articles.title: 主题圆桌: 迎风而立的人，那些漂洋过海寻找的答案｜36氪WAVES2026新浪潮
articles.summary:
“2026年，创投圈的浪潮再次翻涌：AI从技术概念走进产业深水区，硬科技创业从‘小众赛道’ 变成‘主流共识’，年轻的创业者们正在用代码和双手，重新定义中国创新的未来坐标。

每一年，由36氪 · 暗涌主办的WAVES大会，都是中国创投圈的年度风向标。今年的 WAVES 2026以‘今年盛夏’为主题，落地广州番禺良仓新造创意园，在两天的时间里，我们汇聚了顶级投资人、产业领袖、新锐创业者，用14场深度圆桌、数十场独立演讲，拆解 AI、硬科技、出海、医疗等核心赛道的底层逻辑，见证那些‘少数人’ 的坚持，如何汇聚成改变行业的浪潮。”<

articles.summary:
文｜王毓婵

编辑｜张雨忻

先说结论：一些简单操作，小微做得没那么好；一些复杂操作，小微反而做得比较好。

articles.title: 36氪首发 | 同济博士从建筑机器人转战煤矿，押注辅助作业的“结构性蓝海”
articles.summary:
作者丨欧雪

编辑丨袁斯来

硬氪获悉，矿山机器人公司随擎机器人（下称“随擎”）近期已完成千万元级天使轮融资，投资方为连云港金桥基金。本轮资金将主要用于煤矿井下核心机器人产品的工程化迭代、供应链构建及团队扩充。

随擎2025年初正式成立，此前已通过子公司完成近两年的技术积累与井下验证。随擎核心团队深耕智能机器人及特种装备行业多年，平均从业年限超过10年，形成从80后到98后的年龄梯队，核心骨干均有从产品定义到现场交付的全链条实战经历。创始人王龙是同济大学机器人方向博士，国内建筑机器人早期开拓者，曾任香港科技大学高级研究员。
